findings.desc: Anyone can drain approved users' funds because the function allows an arbitrary choice of the `from` address in `safeTransferFrom`.
Lack of signature replay protection (no nonces or tracking of used signatures) allows an attacker to reuse a single valid signature indefinitely to withdraw all funds.
findings.counterexample: assertion fails with no symbolic input (trivial witness)

findings.desc: The function executes arbitrary low-level calls to target addresses, enabling an attacker to trigger the token's `transferFrom` using the bridge's infinite vault approval.
Users can permanently DOS deposits by directly transferring tokens to the vault, artificially inflating the balance beyond the hardcoded `DEPOSIT_LIMIT`.

proposer.raw:
HIGH | L1BossBridge.depositTokensToL2 | Anyone can drain approved users' funds because the function allows an arbitrary choice of the `from` address in `safeTransferFrom`.
HIGH | L1BossBridge.sendToL1 | Lack of signature replay protection (no nonces or tracking of used signatures) allows an attacker to reuse a single valid signature indefinitely to withdraw all funds.
HIGH | L1BossBridge.sendToL1 | The function executes arbitrary low-level calls to target addresses, enabling an attacker to trigger the token's `transferFrom` using the bridge's infinite vault approval.
MEDIUM | L1BossBridge.depositTokensToL2 | Users can permanently DOS deposits by directly transferring tokens to the vault, artificially inflating the balance beyond the hardcoded `DEPOSIT_LIMIT`.

results.finding: a signed withdrawal can be replayed to drain the vault.
results.counterexample: assertion fails with no symbolic input (trivial witness)
